title: Podman Crun not found: invalid argument SSSD/Ldap user
body:
### Issue Description

`podman version
Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument`

Rootless podman fails to run with above error all users that configured via sssd/ldap.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Setup arch linux box with SSSD and LDAP auth following https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/LDAP_authentication#SSSD_configuration
2. Set suid in /etc/subid and /etc/subgid
3. Login with ldap user
4. Attempt to run any podman command.

### Describe the results you received

Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument

### Describe the results you expected

Podman executing.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman info
Error: default OCI runtime "crun" not found: invalid argument
podman --version
podman version 5.8.1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is set the correct location and is writable.
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/11001

### Additional information

Trying to run from a user that is using KDE plasma and SDDM desktop manager.

comments.body:
Forgot to mention that I have crun installed and can run simple commands without issue.
~~~
crun --help
Usage: crun [OPTION...] COMMAND [OPTION...]

COMMANDS:
	checkpoint  - checkpoint a container
	create      - create a container
	delete      - remove definition for a container
	exec        - exec a command in a running container
	features    - show the enabled features
	list        - list known containers
	mounts      - add or remove mounts from a running container
	kill        - send a signal to the container init process
	ps          - show the processes in the container
	restore     - restore a container
	run         - run a container
	spec        - generate a configuration file
	start       - start a container
	state       - output the state of a container
	pause       - pause all the processes in the container
	resume      - unpause the processes in the container
	update      - update container resource constraints

      --cgroup-manager=MANAGER   cgroup manager
      --debug                produce verbose output, similar to
                             --log-level=debug
      --log=FILE             log destination: '[file:]PATH', 'journald:ID' or
                             'syslog:ID' (defaults to stderr)
      --log-format=FORMAT    log format: 'text' (default) or 'json'
      --log-level=LEVEL      log level to use: 'error' (default), 'warning' or
                             'debug'
      --root=DIR
      --rootless=VALUE
      --systemd-cgroup       use systemd cgroups
  -?, --help                 Give this help list
      --usage                Give a short usage message
  -v, -V, --version

Report bugs to https://github.com/containers/crun/issues.

crun -v
crun version 1.27
commit: a718a92cc9a94955a5a550b6fdec1378c247ec50
rundir: /run/user/11001/crun
spec: 1.0.0
+SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +LIBKRUN +YAJL
~~~

where is crun installed?  Can you show the absolute path (`which crun`) and the value of the $PATH env variable (`printenv PATH`)?

If it is not in a standard path, you'll need to configure the correct path in the `containers.conf` file, by default these are the paths used:

```
# Paths to look for a valid OCI runtime (crun, runc, kata, runsc, krun, etc)
[engine.runtimes]
#crun = [
#  "/usr/bin/crun",
#  "/usr/sbin/crun",
#  "/usr/local/bin/crun",
#  "/usr/local/sbin/crun",
#  "/sbin/crun",
#  "/bin/crun",
#  "/run/current-system/sw/bin/crun",
#]
```

~~~
which crun
/usr/bin/crun
~~~

Also tried to run podman as a non-ldap/SSSD user, a user configured completely locally, and I had no issues with rootless podman.

Also, trying to explicitly set the runtime path results in an error where podman thinks I am a different user
~~~
podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun version
Error: creating OCI runtime exit files directory: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied

id -u
11001
~~~

Is the storage used from multiple systems with different settings?

Also please share the output of `podman info` as requested in the template

Here is rootful podman info
~~~

> Is the storage used from multiple systems with different settings?

Do you mean is the storage NFS?  No, the storage is not NFS, the backing filesystem is ZFS.

What ociRuntime block do you get from `podman info` if you run as rootless?

> > Is the storage used from multiple systems with different settings?
> 
> Do you mean is the storage NFS? No, the storage is not NFS, the backing filesystem is ZFS.

ok thanks, was it copied from another system though?

I am still trying to understand how the error `Error: creating OCI runtime exit files directory: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied` could happen.

Could you try with a fresh storage, something like `podman --root /tmp/new-store --runtime /usr/bin/crun run --rm busybox echo hello`?  Do you still get the same error?

> ok thanks, was it copied from another system though?

The file system is not an image of another file system if that is what you are asking.  Also, I have multiple machines configured in the same way, not deployed as images, and they are all having this issue.

> Could you try with a fresh storage, something like podman --root /tmp/new-store --runtime /usr/bin/crun run --rm busybox echo hello? Do you still get the same error?

I do not.

~~~
podman --root /tmp/new-store --runtime /usr/bin/crun run --rm busybox echo hello
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 481282afbc43 done   |
Copying config 925ff61909 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
hello
~~~

So the error is in the way the storage was created.  What I asked is if the directory was copied from another system. In that case it is up to you to make sure everything matches.  Otherwise start with a fresh storage after `podman system reset -f` (this destroys all your containers, images and volumes)

Ran the following but now podman thinks I am someone else.

~~~
sudo podman system reset -f
[sudo] password for keith:
 A "/etc/containers/storage.conf" config file exists.
Remove this file if you did not modify the configuration.

~/ sudo mv /etc/containers/storage.conf{,.bak}

~/ sudo podman system reset -f

~/ podman run --rm busybox echo hello
Error: configure storage: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied

~/ podman system reset -f
Error: configure storage: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
~~~

This users home directory was migrated from a local user to an ldap user, that original local user might have been user 1000.

can it be some config file in `~/.config/containers`?

You need to run `podman system reset -f` as rootless user, otherwise you are destroying the system storage

I do not have a `~/.config/containers` directory.  

Podman thinks I am user 1000 when I run rootless system reset.
~~~
podman system reset -f
Error: configure storage: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
~~~
can you use sudo to remove the rootless storage (`sudo rm -rf  ~/.local/share/containers/`)?

That did it, thank you for the support.  I will close the issue now.

~~~
ls ~/.local/share/containers
cache  storage

sudo rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers

podman system reset -f
ERRO[0000] shutting down container storage: umount /home/keith/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x2: no such file or directory

podman system reset -f

podman run --rm busybox echo hello
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/00-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 481282afbc43 done   |
Copying config 925ff61909 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
hello
~~~
